source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Wind energy will become a large-scale renewable and clean energy source developed in the 21st century. Wind turbines are devices that convert wind energy (the kinetic energy of airflow) into electrical energy. Their main components include wind turbines, gearboxes, generators, etc. As shown in the figure, do not add any other content.

1. The area swept by the rotation of wind turbine blades is the area through which the wind turbine can capture wind energy. Let the air density be $\rho$, the wind speed be $v$, and the length of the wind turbine blades be $r$. Determine the maximum wind energy $P_{m}$ flowing towards the wind turbine per unit time.
2. The electrical power output $P$ of a wind turbine is proportional to $P_{m}$. A certain wind turbine can output an electrical power of $P_{1} = 540 \, \mathrm{kW}$ at a wind speed of $v_{1} = 9 \, \mathrm{m/s}$. In a certain region of China, the wind speed is not less than $v_{2} = 6 \, \mathrm{m/s}$ for approximately 5,000 hours per year. Estimate the minimum annual electricity generation of this wind turbine in the region in kilowatt-hours.

issues: metadata.image_paths 与顶层 image_paths 不一致

image_dependency: question explicitly says 'As shown in the figure' and requires_image=true
text_dominant=false and an image asset is present in the registry

however, the needed quantities and relationships (air density, wind speed, blade length, proportionality, yearly hours) are stated in text, so the figure appears at least partly illustrative rather than strictly necessary
multi_step: the problem has two linked sub-questions: first derive maximum wind power from swept area and wind speed, then use proportionality and a different wind speed to estimate annual generation
solving requires combining geometry/area, kinetic-energy or flow-power reasoning, proportional scaling with wind speed, and time-to-energy conversion
answer_text contains both a symbolic formula and a separate numerical estimate, consistent with multi-step reasoning
verifiability: answer_type is short_text and the expected outputs are a closed-form formula and a numeric annual-energy estimate
both sub-answers are checkable from explicit givens using standard physical relations and unit conversion

risk_flags: figure may be illustrative rather than essential, so image dependence is uncertain
metadata.image_paths 与顶层 image_paths 不一致

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和图像资产均存在，核心语义支撑完整
multi_step_score 和 verifiability_score 较高，具备较好的推理价值和可校验性
虽然存在 metadata.image_paths 与顶层 image_paths 不一致的问题，但 registry_passed=true 且图像文件实际存在，属于可容忍的元数据风险
图像依赖性虽有不确定性，但题目文本已提供主要求解信息，不影响进入候选池

1. The area swept by the rotation of wind turbine blades is the area through which the wind turbine can capture wind energy. Let the air density be $\rho$, the wind speed be $v$, and the length of the wind turbine blades be $r$. Determine the maximum wind energy $P_m$ flowing towards the wind turbine per unit time.
2. The electrical power output $P$ of a wind turbine is proportional to $P_m$. A certain wind turbine can output an electrical power of $P_1 = 540\,\mathrm{kW}$ at a wind speed of $v_1 = 9\,\mathrm{m/s}$. In a certain region of China, the wind speed is not less than $v_2 = 6\,\mathrm{m/s}$ for approximately 5,000 hours per year. Estimate the minimum annual electricity generation of this wind turbine in the region in kilowatt-hours.

open_ended_problem_variants.title: PhysReason 开放题
open_ended_problem_variants.rewritten_question_text:
Wind energy will become a large-scale renewable and clean energy source developed in the 21st century. Wind turbines are devices that convert wind energy (the kinetic energy of airflow) into electrical energy. Their main components include wind turbines, gearboxes, generators, etc. As shown in the figure.

1. The area swept by the rotation of wind turbine blades is the area through which the wind turbine can capture wind energy. Let the air density be $\rho$, the wind speed be $v$, and the length of the wind turbine blades be $r$. Determine the maximum wind energy $P_m$ flowing towards the wind turbine per unit time.
2. The electrical power output $P$ of a wind turbine is proportional to $P_m$. A certain wind turbine can output an electrical power of $P_1 = 540\,\mathrm{kW}$ at a wind speed of $v_1 = 9\,\mathrm{m/s}$. In a certain region of China, the wind speed is not less than $v_2 = 6\,\mathrm{m/s}$ for approximately 5,000 hours per year. Estimate the minimum annual electricity generation of this wind turbine in the region in kilowatt-hours.
